mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month changes in payroll employment signal ongoing economic expansion and rising aggregate income, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as corporate revenue expectations increase.
failure_condition: Months with positive month-over-month PAYEMS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55 percent of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate increase of more than 15 basis points within a quarter after an extended period at or below 25 basis points signals a shift from zero-bound policy to active tightening, which raises discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises >0.15pp within 3 months after prior 3 months at or below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:Effective fed funds rate at 0.39% in July 2016 after near-zero bound
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 50 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer spending and residential investment, which produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days that exceed either single-series signal alone.
failure_condition: Concurrent monthly increases in RSAFS and HOUST are followed by positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55 percent of cases, or an interaction regression shows the product term is not positive and significant at the 5 percent level.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is below 5.0 percent, month-over-month declines signal labor market overheating and imminent wage-push inflation, which triggers monetary policy tightening expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances where UNRATE is below 5.0 percent and declines month-over-month are followed by negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production signals rising manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through higher corporate earnings and operating leverage.
failure_condition: Months with positive month-over-month INDPRO changes are followed by positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55 percent of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent annualized indicates aggregate demand weakness relative to trend, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: Triggered quarters produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 50 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in consumer prices and the federal funds rate create a restrictive real monetary environment that compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Observations following joint month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS show negative 5-day returns in fewer than 52 percent of cases, or the interaction term does not add explanatory power in a joint regression (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Within 12 months following a quarter of negative real GDP growth, month-over-month recoveries in retail sales signal a demand rebound from recessionary conditions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as risk appetite recovers.
failure_condition: RSAFS monthly increases occurring within 12 months of a negative GDPC1 quarter are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 45 percent of instances.
